title: Rewrite SequenceDiagram with CSS variables, framer-motion arrows, and responsive colWidth
description: SequenceDiagram scores 3/24 — worst in module — and is used by 5 visualizers (TCP, TLS, DNS, WebSocket, CDN). Fixing it improves 6 components simultaneously. Changes: (1) Replace 7 hardcoded hex values with CSS variables (var(--gray-4), var(--primary), etc.), (2) Animate arrow reveals using SVG stroke-dashoffset self-drawing technique (300ms ease-out per arrow), (3) Animate opacity with framer-motion (300ms transition instead of instant), (4) Make colWidth dynamic via ResizeObserver: Math.max(120, containerWidth / columns.length), (5) Add font-family: var(--font-sans) to SVG root. Estimated impact: lifts 6 component scores by ~47%.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex values in SequenceDiagram SVG
Each message arrow draws itself from source to destination over 300ms
Opacity transitions smoothly (300ms) instead of instant snap
DNS 5-column diagram renders legibly at 375px viewport
SVG text uses application font stack
All 5 consuming visualizers render correctly after refactor

title: Create shared spring animation presets for consistent motion language
description: Currently animations use inconsistent configs: some use { duration: 0.3 }, some use framer-motion defaults, some use CSS transition-all. Create a shared animation config: SPRING_SNAPPY (stiffness:400, damping:30 — buttons/toggles), SPRING_GENTLE (stiffness:200, damping:25 — panels), SPRING_BOUNCY (stiffness:300, damping:15 — celebrations), SPRING_SLOW (stiffness:100, damping:20 — layout shifts). Source: Apple WWDC 2018 Designing Fluid Interfaces, Josh Comeau spring physics guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared animation config file with 4 named spring presets
All framer-motion animations in PacketJourney and ConnectionPool use presets
All CSS transitions in HTTP/API/Serialization bars replaced with framer-motion springs
Button interactions use SPRING_SNAPPY, panel transitions use SPRING_GENTLE
No bare { duration: X } configs remaining

title: Add staggered entry animations to ConnectionPool tiles and PacketJourney hops
description: Connection pool tiles currently all animate in simultaneously. PacketJourney hops appear one at a time but with no stagger on initial load. Add parent variant with staggerChildren: 0.05 so elements cascade in like a wave. Source: Maxime Heckel framer-motion guide. Apply to: connection tiles on pool initialization, hop items on simulation start, encapsulation stack tags, and comparison bar reveals.
acceptanceCriteria: Connection pool tiles cascade in with 50ms stagger on initialization
PacketJourney hop items stagger on initial render
Encapsulation stack tags stagger as they accumulate
Stagger applies to all list-like visual elements

title: Add sonar-ripple effect to active hop indicator in PacketJourney
description: Current active hop has a tiny 8x8 pulsing dot (scale [1,1.4,1]). Replace with a larger 12x12 dot plus an expanding sonar ripple ring that fades outward — like a radar ping. The ripple draws attention without being distracting. Source: Washington Post election dashboard CHI 2024 paper, Apple notification indicator pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Active hop dot is 12x12 instead of 8x8
Expanding ring animation radiates outward from the dot and fades
Ring color matches the layer color via layerColor()
Animation is smooth at 60fps
Reduced-motion: ring disabled, dot is static

title: Make PacketJourney hop heights time-proportional to latency
description: Currently all hops have equal visual height regardless of latency (20ms DNS vs 50ms App Server look the same). Make hop height proportional to latency so slow steps visually dominate. Min height 32px (for 2ms hops), max 80px (for 50ms+ hops). Scale: height = max(32, min(80, latencyMs * 1.2)). Source: Flourish timeline maker with proportional spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: Hop row heights scale with latency (high latency = taller row)
50ms App Server hop is visually ~2.5x taller than 2ms edge check
Min height ensures small hops remain clickable/readable
Total timeline height adapts to selected protocol (more hops = taller)

title: Add progressive disclosure to CORS decision tree with expand animations
description: Currently all CORS steps appear instantly as a vertical list when stepIndex advances. Replace with progressive disclosure: start with 'Is this same-origin?' and only expand the next decision when the simulation reaches that step. Use framer-motion layout animations for smooth tree expansion. Source: yWorks decision tree demo with progressive reveal + JointJS collapse/expand.
acceptanceCriteria: CORS steps expand one at a time with smooth height/layout animation
Each step card animates in from collapsed (0 height) to expanded
Connector lines animate between steps
Previously revealed steps remain visible but can be collapsed
Expansion uses SPRING_GENTLE preset

title: Add sticky column headers and row hover highlighting to comparison tables
description: API comparison and Serialization feature tables have no sticky headers (protocol names scroll away) and no row hover highlighting. Add: (1) sticky first column with feature names frozen on horizontal scroll, (2) sticky column headers with protocol badges, (3) full-row hover highlight on mouseover for visual tracking. Source: Smashing Magazine comparison table design, NN/Group research-backed table patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Protocol name headers stick when table scrolls vertically
Feature name column sticks when table scrolls horizontally
Full-row hover highlight illuminates all cells in the row
Active/selected column can be highlighted with subtle tint
Works on both API comparison and Serialization feature tables

title: Add mini sparkline history chart to ConnectionPool metrics
description: ConnectionPool only shows current state (active: 5, idle: 2). Add a mini sparkline showing pool utilization over the last 30 simulation ticks. Shows whether the pool is trending toward exhaustion or recovering. Add a utilization gauge (current/max) with color gradient (green <60%, yellow 60-85%, red >85%). Source: Grafana Druid connection pool dashboard, Stripe dashboard sparklines.
acceptanceCriteria: Mini sparkline chart (60px tall) shows utilization history below the grid
Sparkline updates every tick with smooth line animation
Utilization gauge shows current/max with color gradient
History is last 30 ticks (15 seconds at 500ms interval)
Sparkline uses SVG path with stroke animation

title: Add animated gradient glow border for active connections in pool
description: Active connections currently show a plain colored border. Replace with a rotating gradient glow border using CSS @property --angle animation with conic-gradient. The glow communicates 'this connection is alive and processing' far more effectively than a static border. Source: React Flow Turbo Flow demo with rotating gradient borders.
acceptanceCriteria: Active connection tiles have a rotating gradient glow border
Glow color matches var(--state-success) for active state
Animation is pure CSS (no JS) using @property --angle
Reduced-motion: glow is static (no rotation)
Performance: no layout thrash, GPU-composited

title: Add client-server endpoint mini-map to PacketJourney
description: PacketJourney is an abstract vertical hop list with no spatial representation of 'client' and 'server'. Add a mini-map at the top showing Client on the left and Server on the right with a packet icon that physically moves between them as the simulation progresses. The packet travels right during request hops and left during response hops. Source: dabh.github.io/network_animations simple endpoint view.
acceptanceCriteria: Mini-map at top of PacketJourney shows Client ← → Server
Packet icon animates between endpoints as hops progress
Direction matches packetState.direction (request=right, response=left)
Mini-map is compact (40px tall) and doesn't steal space from hop list
Packet position corresponds to current hop in the overall journey

title: Add lifeline activation bars to SequenceDiagram (standard UML pattern)
description: The SequenceDiagram shows dashed lifelines but no activation bars (colored rectangles showing when a participant is 'active' processing a message). Activation bars are standard in UML sequence diagrams and communicate which party is doing work at each moment. Source: js-sequence-diagrams library, ZenUML.
acceptanceCriteria: Activation bars appear on lifelines when a participant is processing
Bar starts when participant receives a message, ends when they respond
Bar color matches the participant's theme color or message type
Self-messages show a brief activation pulse
Bars respect the step-by-step reveal (only show for active/past steps)

title: Consolidate type scale to 4 sizes max (eliminate 7 sub-12px sizes)
description: The module uses 7 different sub-12px font sizes (8px, 9px, 9.5px, 10px, 10.5px, 11px, 12px). Apple uses 3-4 sizes per component. Consolidate to: 10px (minimum, labels/badges), 11px (body text), 12px (section headers), 13px (emphasis). Eliminate text-[8px] and text-[9px] entirely. SVG fonts scale with viewBox — set them to 11px minimum.
acceptanceCriteria: No text-[8px] or text-[9px] classes remain in any networking file
All text uses one of 4 size tiers: 10px, 11px, 12px, 13px
SVG fontSize attributes are 10 minimum (up from 8)
Layout remains compact without overflow
Type scale documented in a comment or constant
